revision.comment: 大分県中津市にある神社
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 中津大神宮 |画像 = [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (1).jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[大分県]][[中津市]]二ノ丁1273-1 |地図国コード = JP |祭神 = [[天照大神|天照大御神]]、[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神]]、[[倭姫命]]、[[アメノウズメ|天宇豆売命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[近代社格制度|旧郷社]] |創建 = 1881年（明治14年） |本殿 = [[神明造]]<ref name="中津大神宮について">[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/about.html 中津大神宮について] 中津大神宮</ref> |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|36|23.1|N|131|11|9.3|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=中津大神宮}} |地図 = }} {{神社 |名称 = 中津神社 |画像 = [[File:Nakatsu Shrine in Nakatsu Castle.jpg|250px]] |所在地 = [[大分県]][[中津市]]二ノ丁1278-10 |地図国コード = JP |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]、[[応神天皇]]、[[仁徳天皇]] |神体 = |社格 = [[近代社格制度|旧郷社]] |創建 = 1883年（明治16年）3月 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |位置 = |地図 = }} '''中津大神宮'''（なかつだいじんぐう）・'''中津神社'''（なかつじんじゃ）は、[[大分県]][[中津市]]二ノ丁にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。
== 祭神 == ; 中津大神宮<ref name="中津大神宮について"/> * [[天照大神|天照大御神]]（あまてらすおおみかみ） * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神]]（とようけのおおみかみ） * [[倭姫命]]（やまとひめのみこと） * [[アメノウズメ|天宇豆売命]]（あめのうずめのみこと）
; 中津神社<ref name="中津神社について"/> * [[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]（すさのお） * [[応神天皇]] * [[仁徳天皇]] : ほか13柱
== 歴史 == === 明治維新期の中津城 === [[File:中津城天守.jpg|thumb|left|中津城の模擬天守]]
文久3年（1863年）8月、[[中津城]]本丸南側に松の御殿が建てられ、[[中津藩]]江戸藩邸から戻った姫君らが居住した<ref name="説明看板">説明看板「松の御殿」中津市、中津の郷土史を語る会</ref>。1871年（明治4年）7月の[[廃藩置県]]後、松の御殿は[[小倉県]]中津市庁舎や[[大分県]]中津支庁舎として使用された<ref name="説明看板"/>。
1877年（明治10年）1月29日に[[西南戦争]]が勃発すると、同年3月31日には西郷軍（薩摩郡）に呼応した旧[[中津藩]]士の[[士族反乱|不平士族]]が[[中津隊]]として隆起し<ref>[https://rekishikaido.php.co.jp/detail/4975 増田宋太郎と西郷隆盛～西南戦争、中津隊の反乱] WEB歴史街道、2018年3月31日</ref>、中津隊は松の御殿を襲撃して焼失させた<ref name="説明看板"/>。
=== 中津大神宮と中津神社の創建 === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|中津神社の鳥居]]
1881年（明治14年）には中津城址に神宮豊前教会が創建され、1883年（明治16年）3月には松の御殿跡地に中津神社が創建された。
神宮豊前教会は[[伊勢神宮]]の分霊を奉迎鎮祭した神社であり、創建当初は[[豊前国|豊前地域]]（[[企救郡]]・[[田川郡]]・[[京都郡]]・[[仲津郡]]・[[築城郡]]・[[上毛郡]]・[[下毛郡]]・[[宇佐郡]]）の参詣者に伊勢神宮の[[神宮大麻]]（[[神札]]）と[[神宮暦]]を頒布していた<ref name="中津大神宮について"/>。その後、神宮教や神宮奉斎会という名称だった時期を経て、戦後の1946年（昭和21年）に中津大神宮に改称された<ref name="中津大神宮について"/>。
中津神社は新魚町の六所神社、片端町の丸山神社（義氏社）、御小屋の稲荷神社、諸町の蛭子神社、八幡大江神社の分霊を合祀した神社である<ref name="中津神社について">[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html 中津神社について] 中津大神宮</ref>。
== 境内 == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|「西南役中津隊之碑」]]
* 「西南役中津隊之碑」<ref name="境内">[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html 境内のご案内] 中津大神宮</ref> - [[西南戦争]]の際に蜂起した[[中津隊]]の碑。 * 「中津隊百年祭記念碑」<ref name="境内"/> * 「恭事神明碑」<ref name="境内"/> * 「御即位奉祝記念碑」<ref name="境内"/> * 「日露記念砲弾碑」<ref name="境内"/> * 「[[松尾芭蕉]]句碑」<ref name="境内"/> * 城井神社（きいじんじゃ） - [[黒田長政]]によって中津城で謀殺された[[城井谷城]]主の[[城井鎮房]]（宇都宮鎮房）を祀っている<ref name="境内"/>。 * 扇城神社（せんじょうじんじゃ） - 城井鎮房の従臣45柱を祀っている<ref name="境内"/>。 * 金刀比羅宮 - 海上安全の神を祀っている<ref name="境内"/>。
== 祭礼 == {{main|中津祇園}} 毎年7月下旬には[[中津祇園]]が開催され、[[神輿]]と祇園車（[[山車]]）の巡行が行われる<ref name="中津祇園とは">[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ 中津祇園とは] 中津祇園</ref>。[[闇無濱神社]]摂社の八坂神社を中心として行われる下祇園、中津神社を中心として行われる上祇園の両者を合わせて中津祇園と呼称され、最古の記録は永享2年（1430年）に遡る<ref name="中津祇園とは"/>。中津祇園は「豊前三大祭」や「大分県三大祇園祭」のひとつに数えられている<ref name="中津祇園とは"/>。1962年（昭和37年）2月27日には大分県選択無形民俗文化財に指定された。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}}
== 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}}
{{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかつたいしんくう}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:中津市の歴史]] [[Category:中津市の建築物]] [[Category:大分県の旧郷社]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|36|22|N|131|11|5.1|E}}}} |所在地 = [[大分県]][[中津市]]二ノ丁1278-10 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|36|22|N|131|11|5.1|E|display=inline}} |地図国コード = JP |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]、[[応神天皇]]、[[仁徳天皇]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[1883年]]（[[明治]]16年）3月 |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = }} '''中津大神宮'''（なかつだいじんぐう）・'''中津神社'''（なかつじんじゃ）は、[[大分県]][[中津市]]二ノ丁にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は郷社。 == 祭神 == ; 中津大神宮 * [[天照大神|天照大御神]]（あまてらすおおみかみ） * [[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神]]（とようけのおおみかみ） * [[倭姫命]]（やまとひめのみこと） * [[アメノウズメ|天宇豆売命]]（あめのうずめのみこと） ; 中津神社 * [[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]（すさのお） * [[応神天皇]] * [[仁徳天皇]] : ほか13柱 == 歴史 == === 明治維新期の中津城 === [[File:中津城天守.jpg|thumb|right|中津城の模擬天守]] [[文久]]3年（[[1863年]]）8月、[[中津城]]本丸南側に松の御殿が建てられ、[[中津藩]]江戸藩邸から戻った姫君らが居住した説明看板「松の御殿」中津市、中津の郷土史を語る会。1871年（明治4年）7月の[[廃藩置県]]後、松の御殿は[[小倉県]]中津市庁舎や[[大分県]]中津支庁舎として使用された。 [[1877年]]（[[明治]]10年）1月29日に[[西南戦争]]が勃発すると、同年3月31日には西郷軍（薩摩郡）に呼応した旧[[中津藩]]士の[[士族反乱|不平士族]]が[[中津隊]]として隆起し[https://rekishikaido.php.co.jp/detail/4975 増田宋太郎と西郷隆盛～西南戦争、中津隊の反乱] WEB歴史街道、2018年3月31日、中津隊は松の御殿を襲撃して焼失させた。 === 中津大神宮と中津神社の創建 === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|中津神社の鳥居]] [[1881年]]（[[明治]]14年）には中津城址に神宮豊前教会が創建され、[[1883年]]（[[明治]]16年）3月には松の御殿跡地に中津神社が創建された。 神宮豊前教会は[[伊勢神宮]]の分霊を奉迎鎮祭した神社であり、創建当初は[[豊前国|豊前地域]]（[[企救郡]]・[[田川郡]]・[[京都郡]]・[[仲津郡]]・[[築城郡]]・[[上毛郡]]・[[下毛郡]]・[[宇佐郡]]）の参詣者に伊勢神宮の[[神宮大麻]]（[[神札]]）と[[神宮暦]]を頒布していた。その後、神宮教や神宮奉斎会という名称だった時期を経て、戦後の1946年（昭和21年）に中津大神宮に改称された。 中津神社は新魚町の六所神社、片端町の丸山神社（義氏社）、御小屋の稲荷神社、諸町の蛭子神社、八幡大江神社の分霊を合祀した神社である[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html 中津神社について] 中津大神宮。 == 境内 == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|「西南役中津隊之碑」]] * 「西南役中津隊之碑」[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html 境内のご案内] 中津大神宮 - [[西南戦争]]の際に蜂起した[[中津隊]]の碑。 * 「中津隊百年祭記念碑」 * 「恭事神明碑」 * 「御即位奉祝記念碑」 * 「日露記念砲弾碑」 * 「[[松尾芭蕉]]句碑」 * 城井神社（きいじんじゃ） - [[黒田長政]]によって中津城で謀殺された[[城井谷城]]主の[[城井鎮房]]（宇都宮鎮房）を祀っている。 * 扇城神社（せんじょうじんじゃ） - 城井鎮房の従臣45柱を祀っている。 * 金刀比羅宮 - 海上安全の神を祀っている。 == 祭礼 == {{main|中津祇園}} 毎年7月下旬には[[中津祇園]]が開催され、[[神輿]]と祇園車（[[山車]]）の巡行が行われる[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ 中津祇園とは] 中津祇園。[[闇無濱神社]]摂社の八坂神社を中心として行われる下祇園、中津神社を中心として行われる上祇園の両者を合わせて中津祇園と呼称され、最古の記録は永享2年（1430年）に遡る。中津祇園は「豊前三大祭」や「大分県三大祇園祭」のひとつに数えられている。1962年（昭和37年）2月27日に[[大分県指定文化財一覧#選択無形民俗文化財|大分県の選択無形民俗文化財]]に選択された。その後の2004年（平成16年）には「中津祇園」として[[大分県指定文化財一覧#無形民俗文化財|大分県指定無形民俗文化財]]に指定された[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ 県指定文化財] 中津市。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかつたいしんくう}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:中津市の歴史]] [[Category:中津市の建築物]] [[Category:大分県の旧郷社]]

adding religious affiliation to the ones with a broken religious affiliation section
adding the pagename so I can so shrine stuff

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|36|22|N|131|11|5.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, Ni-no-chō 1278-10, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33|36|22|N|131|11|5.1|E|display=inline}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} * {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城天守.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|ja|廃藩置県}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|ja|小倉県}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrine]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Ise talismans|ja|神宮大麻}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|ja|松尾芭蕉}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} and {{ill|Yamahoko|ja|山車}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] == Related Items == * {{ill|Seibo Shrine|ja|聖母宮}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{Disambiguation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakatsu Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Daijingū]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Shrine]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]][[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:disambiguation pages requiring recategorization]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]]

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} * {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城天守.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|ja|廃藩置県}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|ja|小倉県}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Ise talismans|ja|神宮大麻}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|ja|松尾芭蕉}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} and {{ill|Yamahoko|ja|山車}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] == Related Items == * {{ill|Seibo Shrine|ja|聖母宮}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{Disambiguation}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nakatsu Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Daijingū]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]]

It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|ja|松尾芭蕉}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} and {{ill|Yamahoko|ja|山車}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] == Related Items == * {{ill|Seibo Shrine|ja|聖母宮}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{Disambiguation}} {{citation needed|Nakatsu Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Daijingū]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|36|22|N|131|11|5.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, Ni-no-chō 1278-10, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33|36|22|N|131|11|5.1|E|display=inline}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} * {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城天守.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|ja|廃藩置県}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|ja|小倉県}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Ise talismans|ja|神宮大麻}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|ja|松尾芭蕉}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} and {{ill|Yamahoko|ja|山車}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] == Related Items == * {{ill|Seibo Shrine|ja|聖母宮}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{Disambiguation}} {{citation needed|Nakatsu Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Daijingū]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]]

A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{Disambiguation}} {{citation needed|Nakatsu Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Daijingū]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]]

{{Shinto Shrine | name = Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (1).jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|36|23.1|N|131|11|9.3|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, Ni-no-chō 1273-1, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33|36|23.1|N|131|11|9.3|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nakatsu Daijingu}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}
{{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1881 (Meiji 14) | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/about.html About Nakatsu Daijingu] Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nakatsu Shrine in Nakatsu Castle.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|36|22|N|131|11|5.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, Ni-no-chō 1278-10, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33|36|22|N|131|11|5.1|E|display=inline}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} * {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城天守.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|ja|廃藩置県}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|ja|小倉県}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Ise talismans|ja|神宮大麻}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|ja|松尾芭蕉}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} and {{ill|Yamahoko|ja|山車}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] == Related Items == * {{ill|Seibo Shrine|ja|聖母宮}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{Disambiguation}} {{citation needed|Nakatsu Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Daijingū]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]]

{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} * {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城天守.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|ja|廃藩置県}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|ja|小倉県}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Ise talismans|ja|神宮大麻}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|ja|松尾芭蕉}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} and {{ill|Yamahoko|ja|山車}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] == Related Items == * {{ill|Seibo Shrine|ja|聖母宮}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{Disambiguation}} {{citation needed|Nakatsu Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Daijingū]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]]

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} * {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城天守.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|ja|廃藩置県}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|ja|小倉県}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Ise talismans|ja|神宮大麻}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|ja|松尾芭蕉}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} and {{ill|Yamahoko|ja|山車}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] == Related Items == * {{ill|Seibo Shrine|ja|聖母宮}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{Disambiguation}} {{citation needed|Nakatsu Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Daijingū]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} * {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城天守.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|ja|廃藩置県}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|ja|小倉県}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Ise talismans|ja|神宮大麻}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|ja|松尾芭蕉}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} and {{ill|Yamahoko|ja|山車}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] == Related Items == * {{ill|Seibo Shrine|ja|聖母宮}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{Disambiguation}} {{citation needed|Nakatsu Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Daijingū]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} * {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城天守.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|ja|廃藩置県}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|ja|小倉県}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Ise talismans|ja|神宮大麻}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|ja|松尾芭蕉}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} and {{ill|Yamahoko|ja|山車}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] == Related Items == * {{ill|Seibo Shrine|ja|聖母宮}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{Disambiguation}} {{citation needed|Nakatsu Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Daijingū]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|36|22|N|131|11|5.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, Ni-no-chō 1278-10, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33|36|22|N|131|11|5.1|E|display=inline}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} * {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城天守.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|ja|廃藩置県}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|ja|小倉県}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Ise talismans|ja|神宮大麻}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|ja|松尾芭蕉}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} and {{ill|Yamahoko|ja|山車}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] == Related Items == * {{ill|Seibo Shrine|ja|聖母宮}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{Disambiguation}} {{citation needed|Nakatsu Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Daijingū]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Unsorted kk]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} * {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城天守.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|ja|廃藩置県}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|ja|小倉県}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Ise talismans|ja|神宮大麻}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|ja|松尾芭蕉}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} and {{ill|Yamahoko|ja|山車}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] == Related Items == * {{ill|Seibo Shrine|ja|聖母宮}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{Disambiguation}} {{citation needed|Nakatsu Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Daijingū]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Unsorted kk]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq-Nakatsu Daijingū-qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shinto Shrine | name = Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (1).jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|36|23.1|N|131|11|9.3|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, Ni-no-chō 1273-1, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33|36|23.1|N|131|11|9.3|E|region:JP-44_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Nakatsu Daijingu}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}
{{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1881 (Meiji 14) | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}}[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/about.html About Nakatsu Daijingu] Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nakatsu Shrine in Nakatsu Castle.jpg|250px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|33|36|22|N|131|11|5.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, Ni-no-chō 1278-10, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33|36|22|N|131|11|5.1|E|display=inline}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu|ja|中津市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} * {{ill|Yamato-hime no Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城天守.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|ja|廃藩置県}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|ja|小倉県}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Ise talismans|ja|神宮大麻}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|ja|松尾芭蕉}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|ja|黒田長政}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}} and {{ill|Yamahoko|ja|山車}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] == Related Items == * {{ill|Seibo Shrine|ja|聖母宮}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{Disambiguation}} {{citation needed|Nakatsu Shrine}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Daijingū]][[simple:user:immanuelle/Nakatsu Shrines]][[Category:shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Unsorted kk]][[Category:unsorted disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]][[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]] [[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū forks]][[Category:qq]][[Category:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Unsorted disambiguation pages]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nakatsu Daijingū]][[Category:qq]]
