Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Shoumogu|ja|聖母宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoumogu|1=Shoumogu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611081}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{disambiguation}} {{translated page|ja|中津大神宮|version=101285054|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] }} [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nakatsu Daijingu}} {{wikidata link|Q125481312}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Nakatsu Daijingu]][[ja:中津大神宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nakatsu Daijingu]][[ja:中津大神宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q125481312]] Wikidata name: Nakatsu Daijingu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (1).jpg | location = [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu]], Ni-no-chō 1273-1, [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|33.606417|N|131.185917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}
[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]
{{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1881 (Meiji 14) | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]][http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/about.html About Nakatsu Daijingu] Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | location = [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu]], Ni-no-chō 1278-10, [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]] | deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) | location = {{ill|Nakatsu|WD=Q861221}} }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城Tenshu.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) [[:en:Abolition of the han system|Abolition of the han system]], the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the [[:en:Kokura Prefecture|Kokura Prefecture]] administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Corps|1=Nakatsu Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366514}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing [[:en:Jingū taima|Ise talismans]] and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * [[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]] Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Tadamune|1=Utsunomiya Shigefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425978}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Gion Festival|qq=}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] and [[:en:Matsuri float|Yamahoko]] processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 闇無浜神社|1=Kuranashihama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q130214370}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Shoumogu|ja|聖母宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoumogu|1=Shoumogu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611081}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{disambiguation}} {{translated page|ja|中津大神宮|version=101285054|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] }} [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nakatsu Daijingu}} {{wikidata link|Q125481312}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Shoumogu|ja|聖母宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoumogu|1=Shoumogu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611081}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. [[commons:Category:Nakatsu Daijingu]][[ja:中津大神宮]] [[commons:Category:Nakatsu Daijingu]][[ja:中津大神宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nakatsu Daijingu]][[ja:中津大神宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q125481312]] Wikidata name: Nakatsu Daijingu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (1).jpg | location = [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu]], Ni-no-chō 1273-1, [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|33.606417|N|131.185917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}
[[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]]
{{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1881 (Meiji 14) | Main Hall Style = [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]][http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/about.html About Nakatsu Daijingu] Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | location = [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu]], Ni-no-chō 1278-10, [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]] | deity = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]]
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) | location = {{ill|Nakatsu|WD=Q861221}} }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]] : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城Tenshu.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) [[:en:Abolition of the han system|Abolition of the han system]], the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the [[:en:Kokura Prefecture|Kokura Prefecture]] administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Corps|1=Nakatsu Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366514}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing [[:en:Jingū taima|Ise talismans]] and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * [[:en:Matsuo Bashō|Matsuo Bashō]] Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Tadamune|1=Utsunomiya Shigefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425978}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by [[:en:Kuroda Nagamasa|Kuroda Nagamasa]]. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Gion Festival|qq=}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]] and [[:en:Matsuri float|Yamahoko]] processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 闇無浜神社|1=Kuranashihama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q130214370}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Shoumogu|ja|聖母宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoumogu|1=Shoumogu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611081}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{disambiguation}} {{translated page|ja|中津大神宮|version=101285054|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]] {{Commons category|Nakatsu Daijingu}} {{wikidata link|Q125481312}} [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Shoumogu|ja|聖母宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoumogu|1=Shoumogu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611081}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{disambiguation}} {{translated page|ja|中津大神宮|version=101285054|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]] {{Commons category|Nakatsu Daijingu}} {{wikidata link|Q125481312}} [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Nakatsu Daijingu]][[ja:中津大神宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Nakatsu Daijingu]][[ja:中津大神宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nakatsu Daijingu]][[ja:中津大神宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q125481312]] Wikidata name: Nakatsu Daijingu [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| | name = Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (1).jpg | location = {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|WD=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu}}, Ni-no-chō 1273-1, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.606417|N|131.185917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}
{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1881 (Meiji 14) | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri}}[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/about.html About Nakatsu Daijingu] Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | location = {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|WD=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu}}, Ni-no-chō 1278-10, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) | location = {{ill|Nakatsu|WD=Q861221}} }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|WD=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城Tenshu.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|en|Abolition of the han system|WD=Q331905|lt=Abolition of the han system}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|en|Kokura Prefecture|WD=Q5765997|lt=Kokura Prefecture}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Corps|1=Nakatsu Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366514}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Jingū taima|en|Jingū taima|WD=Q11589469|lt=Ise talismans}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|WD=Q5676|lt=Matsuo Bashō}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Tadamune|1=Utsunomiya Shigefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425978}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Gion Festival|qq=}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi}} and {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Yamahoko}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 闇無浜神社|1=Kuranashihama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q130214370}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Shoumogu|ja|聖母宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoumogu|1=Shoumogu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611081}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{disambiguation}} {{translated page|ja|中津大神宮|version=101285054|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]] {{Commons category|Nakatsu Daijingu}} {{wikidata link|Q125481312}} [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) | location = {{ill|Nakatsu|WD=Q861221}} }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|WD=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城Tenshu.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|en|Abolition of the han system|WD=Q331905|lt=Abolition of the han system}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|en|Kokura Prefecture|WD=Q5765997|lt=Kokura Prefecture}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Corps|1=Nakatsu Corps|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366514}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Jingū taima|en|Jingū taima|WD=Q11589469|lt=Ise talismans}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|WD=Q5676|lt=Matsuo Bashō}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Tadamune|1=Utsunomiya Shigefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425978}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Gion Festival|qq=}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi}} and {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Yamahoko}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 闇無浜神社|1=Kuranashihama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q130214370}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Shoumogu|ja|聖母宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoumogu|1=Shoumogu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611081}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{disambiguation}} {{translated page|ja|中津大神宮|version=101285054|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]] {{Commons category|Nakatsu Daijingu}} {{wikidata link|Q125481312}} [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Shoumogu|ja|聖母宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoumogu|1=Shoumogu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611081}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{disambiguation}} {{translated page|ja|中津大神宮|version=101285054|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]] {{Commons category|Nakatsu Daijingu}} {{wikidata link|Q125481312}} [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Nakatsu Daijingu]][[ja:中津大神宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Nakatsu Daijingu]][[ja:中津大神宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Nakatsu Daijingu]][[ja:中津大神宮]] {{Infobox religious building| | name = Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (1).jpg | location = {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|WD=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}}, Ni-no-chō 1273-1, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.606417|N|131.185917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}
{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1881 (Meiji 14) | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}}[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/about.html About Nakatsu Daijingu] Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | location = {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|WD=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}}, Ni-no-chō 1278-10, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) | location = {{ill|Nakatsu|WD=Q861221|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}} }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|WD=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城Tenshu.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|en|Abolition of the han system|WD=Q331905|lt=Abolition of the han system|1=Abolition of the han system}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|en|Kokura Prefecture|WD=Q5765997|lt=Kokura Prefecture|1=Kokura Prefecture}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|1=Satsuma Rebellion}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Corps|1=Q11366514|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366514}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Jingū taima|en|Jingū taima|WD=Q11589469|lt=Ise talismans|1=Jingū taima}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|WD=Q5676|lt=Matsuo Bashō|1=Matsuo Bashō}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Tadamune|1=Utsunomiya Shigefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425978}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Gion Festival|qq=}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} and {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Yamahoko|1=Matsuri float}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 闇無浜神社|1=Kuranashihama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q130214370}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Shoumogu|ja|聖母宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoumogu|1=Seibo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611081}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{disambiguation}} {{translated page|ja|中津大神宮|version=101285054|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]] {{Commons category|Nakatsu Daijingu}} {{wikidata link|Q125481312}} [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1881 (Meiji 14) | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}}[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/about.html About Nakatsu Daijingu] Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | location = {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|WD=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}}, Ni-no-chō 1278-10, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) | location = {{ill|Nakatsu|WD=Q861221|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}} }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|WD=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|WD=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城Tenshu.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|en|Abolition of the han system|WD=Q331905|lt=Abolition of the han system|1=Abolition of the han system}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|en|Kokura Prefecture|WD=Q5765997|lt=Kokura Prefecture|1=Kokura Prefecture}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|1=Satsuma Rebellion}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Corps|1=Q11366514|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11366514}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Jingū taima|en|Jingū taima|WD=Q11589469|lt=Ise talismans|1=Jingū taima}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|WD=Q5676|lt=Matsuo Bashō|1=Matsuo Bashō}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Tadamune|1=Utsunomiya Shigefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11425978}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|WD=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Gion Festival|qq=}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} and {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|WD=Q11470974|lt=Yamahoko|1=Matsuri float}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 闇無浜神社|1=Kuranashihama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q130214370}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Shoumogu|ja|聖母宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoumogu|1=Seibo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611081}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{disambiguation}} {{translated page|ja|中津大神宮|version=101285054|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]] {{Commons category|Nakatsu Daijingu}} {{wikidata link|Q125481312}} [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 闇無浜神社|1=Kuranashihama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q130214370}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Shoumogu|ja|聖母宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoumogu|1=Seibo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611081}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|qid=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|1=Satsuma Rebellion}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Corps|1=Q11366514|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11366514}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Jingū taima|en|Jingū taima|qid=Q11589469|lt=Ise talismans|1=Jingū taima}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|qid=Q5676|lt=Matsuo Bashō|1=Matsuo Bashō}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Tadamune|1=Utsunomiya Shigefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11425978}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|qid=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Gion Festival|qq=}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|qid=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} and {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|qid=Q11470974|lt=Yamahoko|1=Matsuri float}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 闇無浜神社|1=Kuranashihama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q130214370}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Shoumogu|ja|聖母宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoumogu|1=Seibo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11611081}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{disambiguation}} {{translated page|ja|中津大神宮|version=101285054|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]] {{Commons category|Nakatsu Daijingu}} {{wikidata link|Q125481312}} [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Nakatsu Daijingu]][[ja:中津大神宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Nakatsu Daijingu]][[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津大神宮]] {{Infobox religious building| | name = Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (1).jpg | location = {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|qid=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}}, Ni-no-chō 1273-1, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.606417|N|131.185917|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}}
{{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}
{{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1881 (Meiji 14) | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}}[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/about.html About Nakatsu Daijingu] Nakatsu Daijingu | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | location = {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|qid=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}}, Ni-no-chō 1278-10, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = 1883 (Meiji 16) | location = {{ill|Nakatsu|qid=Q861221|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}} }} '''Nakatsu Daijingu''' (中津大神宮) and '''Nakatsu Shrine''' (中津神社) are Shinto shrines located in Ni-no-chō, {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|qid=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}. Both shrines hold the status of former village shrines under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == ; Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}} * {{ill|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|en|Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qid=Q4388732|lt=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|天宇豆売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Uzume|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ; Nakatsu Shrine[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/nakatsujinjya.html About Nakatsu Shrine] Nakatsu Daijingu * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} * {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} : Plus 13 other deities == History == === Nakatsu Castle in the Meiji Restoration Period === [[File:中津城Tenshu.jpg|thumb|right|Nakatsu Castle’s reconstructed tenshu]] In August 1863 (Bunkyū 3), the southern part of Nakatsu Castle’s main keep was developed into the “Pine Palace,” which housed princesses returning from the domain’s Edo residence.Informational Signboard “Pine Palace,” Nakatsu City, Historical Society of Nakatsu After the 1871 (Meiji 4) {{ill|Abolition of the han system|en|Abolition of the han system|qid=Q331905|lt=Abolition of the han system|1=Abolition of the han system}}, the Pine Palace was repurposed as the Nakatsu City Hall under the {{ill|Kokura Prefecture|en|Kokura Prefecture|qid=Q5765997|lt=Kokura Prefecture|1=Kokura Prefecture}} administration and later served as the Nakatsu Branch of Ōita Prefecture. During the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|qid=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|1=Satsuma Rebellion}}, dissatisfied samurai from the former Nakatsu Domain formed the {{ill|Nakatsu Corps|ja|中津隊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Corps|1=Q11366514|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11366514}} and burned the Pine Palace on March 31, 1877. === Establishment of Nakatsu Daijingu and Nakatsu Shrine === [[File:Nakatsu Daijingu ac (2).jpg|thumb|Torii of Nakatsu Shrines]] Nakatsu Daijingu was established in 1881 (Meiji 14) as the Buzen Shrine Association, enshrining spirits from {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. Initially, it served as a center for pilgrims from the Buzen region, distributing {{ill|Jingū taima|en|Jingū taima|qid=Q11589469|lt=Ise talismans|1=Jingū taima}} and calendars. It underwent several name changes before being renamed Nakatsu Daijingu in 1946. Nakatsu Shrine was established in 1883 (Meiji 16) on the former site of the Pine Palace. It consolidated deities from six local shrines, including Rokusho Shrine, Maruyama Shrine, and Hachiman Ōe Shrine. == Grounds == [[File:Nakatsu Castle Park ac (2).jpg|thumb|160px|“Nakatsu Corps Memorial”]] The grounds feature several notable monuments:[http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/keidai.html Grounds Overview] Nakatsu Daijingu * “Nakatsu Corps Memorial” - Dedicated to the Nakatsu Corps, which rose during the Satsuma Rebellion. * “Centennial Memorial of Nakatsu Corps” * “Kyōji Shinmei Monument” * “Imperial Accession Memorial” * “Russo-Japanese War Shell Monument” * {{ill|Matsuo Bashō|en|Matsuo Bashō|qid=Q5676|lt=Matsuo Bashō|1=Matsuo Bashō}} Haiku Monument * Kii Shrine - Enshrines {{ill|Kii Tadamune|ja|城井鎮房|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Tadamune|1=Utsunomiya Shigefusa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11425978}}, a lord assassinated at Nakatsu Castle by {{ill|Kuroda Nagamasa|en|Kuroda Nagamasa|qid=Q950496|lt=Kuroda Nagamasa|1=Kuroda Nagamasa}}. * Senjō Shrine - Dedicated to 45 retainers of Kii Tadamune. * Kotohira Shrine - Dedicated to the deity of maritime safety. == Festivals == {{main|{{ill|Nakatsu Gion Festival|ja|中津祇園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsu Gion Festival|qq=}}}} Nakatsu Gion, held annually in late July, features {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|qid=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} and {{ill|Matsuri float|en|Matsuri float|qid=Q11470974|lt=Yamahoko|1=Matsuri float}} processions. The festival is centered on Yasaka Shrine (a subsidiary of {{ill|Yamami Shrine|ja|闇無濱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 闇無浜神社|1=Kuranashihama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q130214370}}) and Nakatsu Shrine, collectively known as Nakatsu Gion. Records trace its origins to 1430 (Eikyō 2). Nakatsu Gion is considered one of the “Three Great Festivals of Buzen” and “Three Major Gion Festivals of Ōita Prefecture.”[https://nakatsu-gion.com/rekishi/ About Nakatsu Gion] Nakatsu Gion It was designated a {{ill|Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|大分県選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Prefecture Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 1962 (Shōwa 37) and upgraded to a Prefectural Intangible Folk Cultural Property in 2004.[https://www.city-nakatsu.jp/doc/2011100115127/ Prefectural Cultural Properties] Nakatsu City == References == {{Reflist|2}} == External Links == {{commonscat|Nakatsu Daijingu}} * {{Official|http://nakatsudaijinguu.jp/}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Related Items == * {{ill|Shoumogu|ja|聖母宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoumogu|1=Seibo Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11611081}} - Located in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture. A leading candidate for the Shikinai Myōjin Taisha Nakatsu Shrine. {{disambiguation}} {{translated page|ja|中津大神宮|version=101285054|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:中津大神宮]] [[ja:中津神社]] {{Commons category|Nakatsu Daijingu}} {{wikidata link|Q125481312}} [[Category:All disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nakatsu, Oita]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:shrine disambiguations]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
